name: Myrtle Beach
h1: Personal Injury Lawyer Serving Myrtle Beach, SC
meta_title: Myrtle Beach Personal Injury Lawyer | Roden Law | Free Consult
meta_description: Injured in Myrtle Beach? Roden Law handles car, truck & pedestrian accident cases on US-17, Hwy 501 & Kings Highway. Free consultation. No fees unless we win.
court: Horry County Circuit Court (15th Judicial Circuit)
roads: US-17 (Kings Highway) is the most dangerous road in Horry County, recording 2,181 motor vehicle accidents in a single year with 20 fatalities and 935 injuries. The US-17 Bypass and SC-544 interchange near Surfside Beach is consistently ranked as the most hazardous intersection in Horry County, with 240 crashes recorded from 2018 to 2020. Highway 501 at Carolina Forest Boulevard is another top-5 dangerous intersection with heavy tourist and commuter traffic creating chaotic merge conditions. The intersection of 38th Avenue North and Kings Highway near Village Square Shopping Center sees high rates of rear-end collisions from distracted driving near businesses and attractions.
hospitals:
Grand Strand Medical Center (Level I Adult Trauma / Level II Pediatric Trauma) — 809 82nd Pkwy, Myrtle Beach, SC 29572 — (843) 692-1000
South Strand Medical Center ER — 5050 US-17 BYP S, Myrtle Beach, SC 29588
Carolina Forest ER (Grand Strand Health) — 2690 Professional Circle, Myrtle Beach, SC 29577
landmarks: Broadway at the Beach, Myrtle Beach Boardwalk & Promenade, SkyWheel, Coastal Grand Mall, The Market Common, Myrtle Beach State Park, Myrtle Beach International Airport, Pelicans Ballpark
service_area: Serving Myrtle Beach, Market Common, Grande Dunes, Arcadian Shores, the Golden Mile, and all communities along the Grand Strand from 82nd Parkway to Surfside Beach.
content_hook:
Myrtle Beach is the largest city on the Grand Strand and one of the most popular tourist destinations on the East Coast, drawing approximately 20 million visitors annually. This massive influx of visitors — most of whom are unfamiliar with local roads — combined with a permanent population of roughly 43,000 creates extremely dangerous driving conditions, particularly along US-17 (Kings Highway) and Highway 501.

Horry County had the second-highest rate of fatal crashes of any county in South Carolina in 2022, with 78 fatal collisions resulting in 80 deaths. US-17 through Myrtle Beach is designated as a high-crash corridor by the South Carolina Department of Public Safety. Roden Law’s Murrells Inlet office at 631 Bellamy Ave. serves all of Myrtle Beach. If you’ve been injured in an accident, call (843) 612-1980 for a free case review.

faqs.question: What should I do after a car accident on Kings Highway (US-17) in Myrtle Beach?
faqs.answer: Call 911 immediately, then move your vehicle out of traffic if it is safe to do so. Exchange insurance information with the other driver and photograph the scene, including traffic signals, lane markings, and any visible damage. Seek medical attention even if you feel fine — adrenaline can mask injuries. Do not give a recorded statement to the other driver’s insurance company. Contact Roden Law at (843) 612-1980 for a free case review.

faqs.question: Which hospital should I go to after an accident in Myrtle Beach?
faqs.answer: Grand Strand Medical Center on 82nd Parkway is the region’s only Level I Adult Trauma Center and Level II Pediatric Trauma Center. For accidents in the south end of Myrtle Beach, the South Strand Medical Center ER on US-17 Bypass South is closer. For accidents near Carolina Forest, the Carolina Forest ER provides 24-hour emergency care.

faqs.question: Can a tourist injured in Myrtle Beach file a personal injury claim?
faqs.answer: Yes. You do not need to be a South Carolina resident to file a personal injury claim for an accident that occurred in the state. South Carolina’s 3-year statute of limitations (S.C. Code § 15-3-530) applies regardless of where you live. Roden Law handles cases for both residents and visitors injured in the Grand Strand area.

faqs.question: How long do I have to file an injury claim after a Myrtle Beach accident?
faqs.answer: South Carolina has a 3-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims (S.C. Code § 15-3-530). You must file your lawsuit within 3 years of the date of your accident. However, evidence deteriorates quickly in a tourist-heavy area — surveillance footage is often overwritten and witnesses leave town. Contact an attorney as soon as possible.

name: Conway
h1: Personal Injury Lawyer Serving Conway, SC
meta_title: Conway Personal Injury Lawyer | Roden Law | Free Consult
meta_description: Injured in Conway? Roden Law handles accident cases on Hwy 501, SC-544 & Church Street. Horry County seat. Free consultation. No fees unless we win.
court: Horry County Circuit Court (15th Judicial Circuit)
roads: Highway 501 is the primary corridor between Conway and Myrtle Beach, carrying extremely heavy traffic and experiencing frequent rear-end and head-on collisions, especially during peak tourist season. SC-544 (Dick Pond Road) runs from the Surfside Beach area through Socastee to Conway and was recently expanded but continues to see high crash rates at its major intersections. The intersection of SC-22 and SC-90 near Conway was the site of a fatal high school student crash in early 2026, prompting a petition for a traffic light. Church Street through downtown Conway handles commuter traffic on roads designed for a much smaller population.
hospitals:
Conway Medical Center — 300 Singleton Ridge Rd, Conway, SC 29526 — (843) 347-7111
Grand Strand Health Rivertown ER (opening 2026) — 1720 Church St, Conway, SC 29526
landmarks: Coastal Carolina University, Horry County Courthouse, Conway Riverwalk, Horry County Museum, Downtown Conway Historic District, Waccamaw River, Horry-Georgetown Technical College
service_area: Serving Conway, Coastal Carolina University area, Red Hill, Bucksport, and surrounding communities in central Horry County.
content_hook:
Conway is the county seat of Horry County and home to approximately 26,000 residents, plus the student population of Coastal Carolina University. While Conway retains its historic small-town charm along the Waccamaw River, its roadways bear the burden of being a major feeder to the Myrtle Beach tourist corridor.

Highway 501 between Conway and Myrtle Beach is one of the most congested and dangerous corridors in the region, with traffic volumes that spike dramatically during tourist season. Two Coastal Carolina University students were hit by a vehicle near campus in early 2026, the same stretch of road where the university is building a $10 million pedestrian overpass. Roden Law’s attorneys serve all of Conway from our Murrells Inlet office. Call (843) 612-1980 for a free consultation.

faqs.question: Where are personal injury cases from Conway filed?
faqs.answer: Personal injury lawsuits from Conway are filed in Horry County Circuit Court, which is part of South Carolina’s 15th Judicial Circuit. The courthouse is located in downtown Conway. Our attorneys practice regularly in Horry County courts.

faqs.question: I was hit by a car near Coastal Carolina University — what should I do?
faqs.answer: Call 911 and seek medical treatment at Conway Medical Center. Pedestrian injuries near CCU are unfortunately common due to the volume of traffic on University Boulevard and surrounding roads. Document the scene, get witness contact information, and do not give statements to the driver’s insurance company. Contact Roden Law for a free case review.

faqs.question: How long do I have to file after a Conway car accident?
faqs.answer: South Carolina’s 3-year statute of limitations (S.C. Code § 15-3-530) applies. You must file your lawsuit within 3 years of the accident date. However, contacting an attorney early strengthens your case — evidence from Conway’s traffic cameras and business surveillance systems is often overwritten within weeks.

The city’s stretch of US-17 carries both local commuters and millions of annual visitors, creating dangerous conditions at major intersections.

Main Street in the Ocean Drive section is particularly dangerous for pedestrians during the shag dancing season and summer months. Roden Law serves all of North Myrtle Beach from our Murrells Inlet office. Call (843) 612-1980 for a free case review.

faqs.question: Which hospital is closest to North Myrtle Beach for accident injuries?
faqs.answer: McLeod Seacoast Hospital at 4000 Highway 9 East in Little River is the closest full-service hospital to most of North Myrtle Beach. For the most severe trauma, Grand Strand Medical Center in Myrtle Beach is the region’s only Level I Trauma Center, approximately 15 minutes south.

faqs.question: I was injured as a pedestrian on Main Street in Ocean Drive — do I have a case?
faqs.answer: Likely yes. South Carolina law requires drivers to exercise due care to avoid pedestrians. If a driver was speeding, distracted, or failed to yield at a crosswalk, they may be liable for your injuries. The Ocean Drive area has documented pedestrian safety issues. Contact Roden Law for a free evaluation of your case.

faqs.question: What is the statute of limitations for a North Myrtle Beach accident?
faqs.answer: South Carolina’s 3-year statute of limitations (S.C. Code § 15-3-530) applies. Your lawsuit must be filed within 3 years of the accident date. Contact an attorney promptly to preserve evidence and protect your rights.

name: Carolina Forest
h1: Personal Injury Lawyer Serving Carolina Forest, SC
meta_title: Carolina Forest Personal Injury Lawyer | Roden Law | Free Consult
meta_description: Injured in Carolina Forest? Roden Law handles accident cases on Hwy 501, Carolina Forest Blvd & International Dr. Free consultation. No fees unless we win.
court: Horry County Circuit Court (15th Judicial Circuit)
roads: The intersection of Highway 501 and Carolina Forest Boulevard is one of the most dangerous in the Myrtle Beach metro, with over 10% of survey respondents identifying it as the worst intersection in the area. Multiple left turn lanes and heavy traffic create hazardous conditions, and the intersection appeared on the SCDPS list of the five worst intersections in Horry County in both 2018 and 2020. International Drive carries increasing residential traffic as the master-planned community continues rapid growth. The SC-31 (Carolina Bays Parkway) corridor provides faster access to Myrtle Beach but sees high-speed crashes at its interchanges.
hospitals:
Carolina Forest ER (Grand Strand Health) — 2690 Professional Circle, Myrtle Beach, SC 29577

service_area: Serving Carolina Forest, Tanger Outlets area, Forestbrook, and surrounding master-planned communities along the Highway 501 and SC-31 corridors.
content_hook:
Carolina Forest is one of the fastest-growing communities in Horry County, a master-planned area of approximately 16,500 residents located between Myrtle Beach and Conway along Highway 501. Nearly half of the 25-square-mile area has been set aside for conservation, but the roads serving it were not designed for the explosive population growth the community has experienced.

The Highway 501 and Carolina Forest Boulevard intersection has been identified by the SCDPS as one of the five worst intersections in Horry County, with complaints of drivers running red lights, cutting others off, and dangerously long wait times. A 30-acre wildfire in the Carolina Forest area in early 2026 caused additional road closures and hazards. Roden Law’s Murrells Inlet attorneys serve Carolina Forest residents. Call (843) 612-1980 for a free consultation.

faqs.question: Why is the Highway 501 and Carolina Forest Boulevard intersection so dangerous?
faqs.answer: This intersection combines heavy commuter traffic from Carolina Forest’s growing residential population with tourist traffic heading to and from Myrtle Beach. Multiple left turn lanes create confusion, and drivers frequently run red lights or make unsafe lane changes. The SCDPS has flagged this intersection as one of the worst in Horry County in multiple years.

faqs.question: Where is the nearest emergency room to Carolina Forest?
faqs.answer: The Carolina Forest ER, a Grand Strand Health facility at 2690 Professional Circle, offers 24-hour emergency care within Carolina Forest itself. Conway Medical Center is approximately 10 minutes west on Highway 501. For severe trauma, Grand Strand Medical Center in Myrtle Beach is the region’s Level I Trauma Center.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to hire a personal injury lawyer for a Carolina Forest accident?
faqs.answer: Nothing upfront. Roden Law works on a contingency fee basis — we only get paid if we recover compensation for you. If there is no recovery, there is no fee. Call (843) 612-1980 for a free, no-obligation case review.

content_hook:
Surfside Beach proudly calls itself “The Family Beach” and was designated as the first autism-friendly travel destination in 2016. With approximately 4,800 year-round residents, this small community draws families with children who walk, bike, and play near active roadways during tourist season.

The US-17 Bypass and SC-544 intersection adjacent to Surfside Beach is the single most dangerous intersection in Horry County, with 240 crashes and 73 injuries or fatalities recorded in just three years. The family-oriented nature of Surfside Beach means that pedestrian and bicycle accidents involving children are a particular concern. Roden Law’s Murrells Inlet office is just minutes south of Surfside Beach. Call (843) 612-1980 for a free case review.

faqs.question: Why is the US-17 Bypass / SC-544 intersection near Surfside Beach so dangerous?
faqs.answer: This intersection handles massive volumes of traffic flowing between the Grand Strand’s beach communities and inland destinations. The nearby shopping centers at SayeBrook Town Center and South Strand Commons generate additional turning traffic. High speeds on the Bypass combined with congested intersection approaches create frequent T-bone and rear-end collisions.

faqs.question: My child was injured in a pedestrian accident in Surfside Beach — what are our options?
faqs.answer: South Carolina law allows parents to file a personal injury claim on behalf of a minor child. If a driver struck your child due to negligence — speeding, distracted driving, failure to yield — you may be entitled to compensation for medical bills, pain and suffering, and other damages. The statute of limitations for a minor’s claim does not begin until they turn 18, but early action preserves critical evidence.

faqs.question: How close is Roden Law to Surfside Beach?
faqs.answer: Our Murrells Inlet office at 631 Bellamy Ave. is approximately 5 minutes south of Surfside Beach on US-17. All Surfside Beach personal injury cases are filed in Horry County Circuit Court, where our attorneys practice regularly.

Atlantic Avenue through Garden City Beach was not designed for the traffic volumes it now handles during peak season. Pedestrian accidents are a particular concern as families walk between rental homes and the beach. Roden Law’s Murrells Inlet office is just minutes away. Call (843) 612-1980 for a free case review.

Document the hazard with photos, report the incident, and contact Roden Law for a free evaluation.

name: Little River

meta_title: Little River Personal Injury Lawyer | Roden Law | Free Consult
meta_description: Injured in Little River? Roden Law handles accident cases on Hwy 17, Hwy 9 & Sea Mountain Hwy. Serving northern Horry County. Free consultation.
court: Horry County Circuit Court (15th Judicial Circuit)
roads: US-17 through Little River carries heavy cross-border traffic between South Carolina and North Carolina, with commercial truck traffic adding to the danger. SC Highway 9 runs through the northern part of the community and its intersection with SC-57 was identified as the most dangerous intersection in Horry County with 49 crashes between 2020 and 2024. content_hook:
Little River is a historic fishing village of approximately 4,500 residents at the northern tip of the Grand Strand, near the North Carolina border. Once a pirate harbor in the 1700s, today Little River sits at the crossroads of US-17 cross-border traffic and SC Highway 9 — a combination that produces frequent and serious motor vehicle accidents.

The SC-9 and SC-57 intersection near Little River recorded more crashes than any other intersection in Horry County between 2020 and 2024. Grand Strand Health is constructing a new standalone emergency department in Little River to address the growing need. Roden Law serves all of the northern Grand Strand from our Murrells Inlet office. Call (843) 612-1980 for a free consultation.

faqs.question: I was in an accident near the SC/NC border — which state’s laws apply?
faqs.answer: The state where the accident physically occurred determines which laws apply. If the accident happened on the South Carolina side of the border, South Carolina’s 3-year statute of limitations and modified comparative fault rules apply, even if you are a North Carolina resident. Our attorneys can help determine the correct jurisdiction.

faqs.question: Where is the nearest hospital to Little River?
faqs.answer: McLeod Seacoast Hospital at 4000 Highway 9 East in Little River is the nearest full-service hospital with a 24-bed emergency department. Grand Strand Health is also building a new standalone ER in Little River, expected to open in 2026.

faqs.question: Does Roden Law handle cases in northern Horry County?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our attorneys serve all of Horry County, including Little River, Longs, Wampee, and communities near the North Carolina border. All cases are filed in Horry County Circuit Court in Conway.

Seek medical attention even if your injuries seem minor. South Carolina premises liability law requires businesses to maintain reasonably safe conditions for visitors. With a permanent population of roughly 4,000, the island and its mainland surroundings attract visitors and retirees seeking a quieter alternative to Myrtle Beach.

US-17 through the Pawleys Island corridor handles significant traffic, and the narrow causeway bridges connecting the island to the mainland create bottlenecks where accidents can have serious consequences due to the lack of shoulders or escape routes. faqs.answer: Tidelands Waccamaw Community Hospital in Murrells Inlet is approximately 10 minutes north and provides 24-hour emergency care. Tidelands Georgetown Memorial Hospital in Georgetown is approximately 15 minutes south and also offers 24-hour emergency services.

faqs.question: What if I was partially at fault for my Pawleys Island accident?
faqs.answer: South Carolina uses a modified comparative fault rule — you can recover damages as long as you are less than 51% at fault. Your compensation is reduced by your percentage of fault. For example, if you are 20% at fault on a $100,000 claim, you recover $80,000.

US-17 traffic between Myrtle Beach and Charleston funnels through Georgetown, and the Sampit River bridge creates a chokepoint where congestion-related crashes are common. faqs.answer: Tidelands Georgetown Memorial Hospital is a 131-bed hospital providing 24-hour emergency care and is certified as an advanced primary stroke center.
